payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Claim task completion function enabling children to record work done
payload.justification: Signal describes the core business capability for children to claim they have completed earning tasks.

payload.description: Task specification function enabling parents to define work and rewards
payload.justification: Signal identifies the business capability for parents to specify tasks with associated monetary values.

payload.description: Task viewing function for children to see available and completed work
payload.justification: Signal describes business capability enabling children to view both available tasks and their own completions.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation process summarizing child task completion rewards
payload.justification: Signal defines a business process that aggregates and reports earnings at the end of each weekly period.

payload.description: Payment reconciliation process enabling parent review of child earnings
payload.justification: Signal identifies a business process where parents view earnings reports for payment reconciliation purposes.

payload.description: Task completion events must be associated with the completing child
payload.justification: Signal explicitly states a conceptual relationship rule governing how completions are linked to children.

payload.description: All tasks must return to available state at weekly reset
payload.justification: Signal defines a business rule governing task state transitions during the weekly cycle reset.

payload.description: Child as a business entity participating in the pocket money system
payload.justification: Multiple signals clearly establish Child as a distinct business entity that claims tasks, completes work, views tasks, and accumulates earnings.

payload.description: Parent as a business entity managing the pocket money system
payload.justification: Signals identify Parent as a business entity that specifies tasks and views reports for reconciliation purposes.

payload.description: Task as a work unit entity with monetary reward value
payload.justification: Task is clearly a core business entity that is specified, claimed, completed, viewed, and reset across multiple signals.

payload.description: Task completion event as a business fact entity
payload.justification: Completion events are identified as entities that must be associated with children and retained as historical records.

payload.description: Monetary reward value associated with tasks
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies reward values as data attributes of task entities.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate calculated per child
payload.justification: Earnings are data attributes aggregated from task completions for each child within the weekly period.

payload.description: Task completion status indicating available or completed state
payload.justification: Signals describe tasks having states (available vs completed) that change through the weekly cycle.

payload.description: Association between task completion and completing child
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines a required conceptual relationship linking completion events to child entities.

payload.description: Ownership relationship between parent and specified tasks
payload.justification: Parents specifying tasks implies a business relationship between parent entity and task entity.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governing task availability and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Multiple signals establish a weekly cycle as the fundamental temporal structure for task claiming, earnings aggregation, and reset.

payload.description: Task completion event occurring when child claims work done
payload.justification: Signals describe task completion as a business event that occurs during the weekly cycle and must be associated with the child.

payload.description: Weekly period end event triggering earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Signal identifies period end as a business event that triggers the earnings reporting process.

payload.description: Weekly reset trigger returning all tasks to available state
payload.justification: Signal defines a weekly reset as a timing trigger that causes tasks to transition to available state.

payload.description: Child role performing task completion and earnings accumulation
payload.justification: Signals identify Child as a business role that claims tasks, views work, and receives earnings.

payload.description: Parent role managing task specification and payment reconciliation
payload.justification: Signals identify Parent as a business role responsible for specifying tasks and reviewing earnings for payment.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core business concept establishes the fundamental goal of the system to enable earning through task completion.

payload.description: Provide parents with visibility for payment reconciliation
payload.justification: Signal identifies payment reconciliation as a business objective supported by parent viewing of earnings reports.

payload.description: Task completion history must be retained beyond current period
payload.justification: Signal explicitly mandates retention and accessibility of historical completion data as a business constraint.

payload.description: Task availability operates on a recurring weekly cycle basis
payload.justification: Weekly reset cycle represents a business principle governing how task availability is structured temporally.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation calculation at period boundary
payload.justification: Signals specify logical aggregation process calculating weekly earnings, representing a conceptual process that transforms or consolidates data.

payload.description: Task availability reset execution at weekly cycle boundary
payload.justification: Signals describe logical state transition resetting task availability, indicating a process that modifies system state cyclically.

payload.description: Temporal task record persistence and retrieval
payload.justification: Signals require logical persistence and retrieval mechanism, indicating a function that manages task record lifecycle.

payload.description: Reporting view generation correlating child, task, and earnings data
payload.justification: Signals define logical reporting view, indicating a function that produces analytical output by correlating multiple data elements.

payload.description: Task completion record linking child entities to completed tasks
payload.justification: Signals describe logical association mechanism between completed tasks and child entities, indicating a data entity that captures this relationship at the conceptual level.

payload.description: Weekly earnings calculation aggregate
payload.justification: Signals specify aggregation process calculating weekly earnings, indicating a derived data entity that stores or represents accumulated earnings.

payload.description: Temporal task record maintained for historical reference
payload.justification: Signals require persistence and retrieval of temporal task records, indicating a logical entity that captures task state over time.

payload.description: Association between child entity, completed task, and earnings data (in reporting view)
payload.justification: Signals explicitly describe logical association mechanism linking completed tasks to child entities, representing a conceptual relationship. | Signals define logical reporting view that correlates three data elements, indicating a complex relationship structure.

payload.description: Weekly period boundary occurrence
payload.justification: Signals specify aggregation at period boundary, indicating a temporal event that marks the end of a weekly cycle.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle
payload.justification: Signals describe state transition at weekly cycle boundaries, explicitly indicating a recurring temporal cycle governing task availability.

payload.description: Weekly cycle boundary triggering task reset state transition
payload.justification: Signals describe state transition resetting task availability at cycle boundaries, indicating a trigger condition that initiates the reset process.

payload.justification: Signal describes platform compatibility requirement specifying target operating system versions. payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint
payload.justification: All three Signals describe platform compatibility requirements at Builder level, representing technical constraints on which operating system versions are supported for deployment. Confidence matches Signal confidence at 0.85 as this is explicitly normative content defining compatibility boundaries.

payload.description: Task completion linking procedure
payload.justification: Signals describe operational linking action triggered by task completion event, representing a detailed procedural specification.

payload.description: Weekly reporting cycle for children's earned totals visibility
payload.justification: Signals establish weekly reporting schedule timing for viewing earned totals, representing a detailed recurring cycle specification. | Signals establish a weekly schedule timing for children to view earned totals, representing a recurring reporting cycle specification.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle for task availability reset
payload.justification: Signals specify weekly operational cycle for task reset timing, representing a detailed recurring cycle specification. | Signals specify a weekly schedule for task availability reset, clearly representing a recurring operational cycle with defined timing.

payload.description: Task completion event triggering linking action
payload.justification: Signals describe a timing event (task completion) that triggers an operational linking action, clearly representing a detailed event specification.

payload.description: Weekly time boundary cycle for task status visibility
payload.justification: Signals define weekly time boundaries within which children can view task status, representing a recurring temporal cycle for operational visibility.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node with version requirements
payload.justification: Signals specify operational deployment platform locations including iOS as a distinct named platform target with version requirements. | Signals specify operational deployment platform locations including Android as a distinct named platform target with version requirements.

payload.description: Operational deployment platform node with version requirements
payload.justification: Signals specify deployment platform locations and version requirements for system execution, representing detailed network and deployment node specifications. No distinct named platforms identified to warrant separate CCIs.

payload.description: Child user role performing task claiming operations
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as operational actors with task claiming responsibilities throughout weekly cycles, representing a distinct implementer-level role specification.

payload.description: Parent user role responsible for task definition and value assignment
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify parents as operational actors with task management and monetary assignment responsibilities, representing a distinct implementer-level role specification.

payload.description: Child user role with task status visibility permissions within weekly boundaries
payload.justification: Signals define operational visibility scope for children's task status access, representing a detailed role permission specification.

payload.description: Parent user role providing earned pocket money reports and disbursement to children
payload.justification: Signals define parents' operational reporting role with specific timing requirements, representing a detailed role responsibility specification. | Signals define parents' operational reporting role with specific timing for providing earned pocket money, representing a detailed role specification with defined responsibilities.

payload.description: Child user performing task claiming operations
payload.justification: Signals identify children as operational actors who perform task claiming activity, representing a detailed user interface actor specification.

payload.description: Parent user defining tasks and assigning monetary values
payload.justification: Signal identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task definition and value assignment, representing a detailed actor specification with specific operational responsibilities.

payload.description: Historical task data retention timing requirement
payload.justification: Signals mandate retention timing requirements for operational data, representing a detailed validation constraint. | Signals mandate retention timing for historical task data, representing a detailed validation and retention constraint on operational data.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the work units offered within the pocket money system, including their monetary value, availability cycles, and reset obligations across weekly periods.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for recording and preserving the factual claims of work performed by participants, establishing persistent associations between completion events and the responsible child, and ensuring historical integrity beyond current operating cycles.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for periodic aggregation of earned value attributable to each child participant, enabling parental oversight and reconciliation of payment obligations arising from completed work within defined operating cycles.

payload.name: Task Completion Tracking Model
payload.description: Logical structure and persistence behavior for capturing task completion events, maintaining historical task records, and establishing relationships between children and completed task instances for audit and reference purposes.

payload.name: Weekly Earnings Derivation Logic
payload.description: Logical model for deriving and aggregating earnings data over weekly periods, including the structural representation of earnings aggregates and the transformation logic executed at period boundaries to produce calculated values.

payload.name: Weekly Task Reset Lifecycle
payload.description: Logical state management model governing task availability states through weekly cycles, including the temporal event model that triggers state transitions and the process logic that executes reset behavior at cycle boundaries.

payload.name: Child-Task-Earnings Reporting View Model
payload.description: Logical correlation structure and view generation behavior for integrating child entities, completed tasks, and earnings data into cohesive reporting perspectives, enabling cross-entity analytical queries.

payload.name: Operating System Platform Configuration
payload.description: Physical platform specification defining the target operating system deployment environment and version compatibility requirements that govern where the system can be built and deployed.

payload.name: Task Completion Workflow Specification
payload.description: Detailed specification of task completion event handling, linking procedures, and triggering actions that govern how task state transitions are processed and coordinated at runtime.

payload.name: Platform Deployment Configuration
payload.description: Precise deployment node specifications defining exact platform versions, runtime requirements, and infrastructure parameters for iOS mobile and operational deployment environments.

payload.name: User Role Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed specifications of user role permissions, interaction boundaries, and operational capabilities for parent and child actors including task claiming, definition, value assignment, visibility permissions, reporting, and disbursement operations.

payload.name: Weekly Cycle Timing Specification
payload.description: Exact timing parameters and cycle durations governing weekly operational boundaries for task availability reset, earned totals reporting, and status visibility windows that control system temporal behavior.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a Task as a work unit entity with an associated monetary reward value.
payload.rationale: Defines the core structural entity and its essential monetary attribute that the business must maintain within the task stewardship domain.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a completion status for each task indicating whether the task is available or completed.
payload.rationale: Defines the state attribute required to govern task lifecycle within the weekly operational cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain an ownership relationship between each parent and their specified tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural relationship that governs task accountability and parental authority over task specification.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly operational cycle governing task availability.
payload.rationale: Establishes the temporal governance rule constraining task availability to operate on a recurring weekly basis, derived from the temporal cycle and the principle that underpins it.

payload.statement: The business shall return all tasks to available state at each weekly reset.
payload.rationale: Expresses the business responsibility to execute the state transition triggered by the weekly reset cycle, implementing the task availability governance rule.

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance naming the black-box capability provided at the boundary to enable child claiming actions. Complementary to the actor-action statement above.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion event.
payload.rationale: Business-subject responsibility to preserve task completion as a business fact entity (What). Expresses the behind-boundary obligation to record completion events.

payload.statement: The business shall associate each task completion event with the completing child.
payload.rationale: Business constraint enforcing the structural relationship (What) and the business rule (How) that completion events must carry child attribution. This is a governance obligation on data integrity.

payload.statement: The business shall retain task completion history beyond the current operating period.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint on historical data retention expressing the business governance rule for persistence beyond current cycles.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to support the earning capability that fulfills the system's motivating goal (Why). This is the behind-boundary enablement of the earn-through-work model.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a child as a participating business entity within the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child as a structural entity with the associated role of task completion and earnings accumulation at the business level.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a parent as a participating business entity within the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the parent as a structural entity with the associated role of task specification and payment reconciliation at the business level.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the weekly earnings aggregate artefact, derived from the weekly earnings aggregation process that summarizes child task completion rewards.

payload.statement: The system shall make a child's earnings visible to the parent for payment reconciliation.
payload.rationale: Complementary system affordance enabling the parent's review capability, providing visibility for payment reconciliation at the system boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly operational cycle governing task availability and earnings calculation.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule establishing the temporal constraint on business operations, derived from the weekly cycle CCI.

payload.statement: The business shall perform earnings aggregation at the end of each weekly period.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for executing earnings aggregation triggered by the weekly period end event.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks, specifying the work and associated rewards.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-002 describes a task specification function (How-column) enabling parents to define work and rewards. At Row 2, this is an ACTOR subject statement — the parent is an external party interacting WITH the system (boundary-crossing action). The verb 'define' is the parent's real action. The statement names what the parent does through the system boundary, not how the system realizes it.

payload.statement: A child can view available and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-003 describes a task viewing function (How-column) for children to see work. At Row 2, this is an ACTOR subject statement — the child is an external party interacting WITH the system (boundary-crossing). The verb 'view' is the child's real action. The object 'available and completed tasks' captures both states mentioned in the source CCI.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise task completion events when a child claims work done.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-When-002 describes a task completion event (When-column) occurring when a child claims work done. At Row 2, this is a BUSINESS subject responsibility — the business must recognise and respond to this event trigger behind the boundary. The statement captures the business's obligation to handle the completion event without specifying the technical mechanism (Row 3 HOW).

payload.statement: The system shall persist a task completion record entity linking each child entity to the completed task instance.
payload.rationale: Refines the Row 2 requirement to record task completion events by specifying the logical entity structure that captures the completion relationship.
payload.fit_criteria: Task completion record entity exists with mandatory foreign keys to child and task entities

payload.statement: The system shall associate each task completion record with the identifier of the child who completed the task.
payload.rationale: Refines the business constraint by specifying that the task completion record entity must contain a child identifier reference.
payload.fit_criteria: Every task completion record contains a non-null child entity identifier

payload.statement: The system shall maintain temporal task records for historical reference beyond the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: Refines the retention constraint by specifying the temporal task record entity and its persistence function.
payload.fit_criteria: Temporal task records remain retrievable for all historical weekly periods

payload.statement: The system shall execute a weekly earnings aggregation calculation process at each weekly period boundary.
payload.rationale: Refines the aggregation requirement by specifying the calculation process and its temporal trigger at period boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings aggregation process executes within 5 minutes of each weekly period boundary event

payload.statement: The system shall persist a weekly earnings calculation aggregate entity for each child.
payload.rationale: Refines the weekly earnings aggregate maintenance requirement by specifying the logical entity structure.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly earnings aggregate entity exists with foreign key to child entity and calculated earnings value

payload.statement: The system shall execute a task availability reset process at the weekly cycle boundary to transition all tasks to available state.
payload.rationale: Refines the reset requirement by specifying the process execution triggered by the weekly cycle boundary event.
payload.fit_criteria: All task entities transition to available state within 5 minutes of weekly cycle boundary

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a weekly task availability reset cycle governing task state transitions.
payload.rationale: Refines the operational cycle constraint by specifying the weekly reset cycle governing task availability.
payload.fit_criteria: Task state transitions occur only at weekly cycle boundaries

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a weekly task availability reset cycle and weekly period boundary for earnings calculation.

payload.fit_criteria: Task reset and earnings calculation both execute at the same weekly period boundary

payload.fit_criteria: Reporting view displays child identifier, completed tasks, and weekly earnings aggregate for parent access

payload.statement: The system shall create a task completion record entity when a child claims task completion.

payload.fit_criteria: Task completion record created within 1 second of child claim submission

payload.statement: The system shall persist temporal task records to support historical retrieval operations.
payload.rationale: Refines the retention constraint by specifying the persistence and retrieval functions for temporal task records.
payload.fit_criteria: Temporal task records retrievable for all periods since system inception

payload.statement: The system shall calculate the weekly earnings aggregate by summing reward values from all task completion records associated with each child within the weekly period.

payload.fit_criteria: Weekly earnings aggregate equals sum of reward values from child's task completion records for the period

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between each task completion record and the child entity that completed the task.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between task completion records and child entities, enabling traceability of which child performed which task.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain task records with temporal attributes for historical reference.
payload.rationale: Establishes the temporal task record as a logical entity with time-based attributes to support historical queries and audit trails.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task records and make them retrievable for historical reference.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical persistence and retrieval behavior required to maintain and access temporal task records over time.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical aggregate entity for weekly earnings comprising period identifier, aggregate values, and calculation state.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical structure of the weekly earnings aggregate entity, establishing the compositional elements required to represent calculated earnings data over a weekly period.

payload.statement: The system shall derive weekly earnings aggregate values by executing aggregation decision logic over the earnings data associated with the weekly period.
payload.rationale: Expresses the logical transformation process that produces the weekly earnings aggregate from source earnings data, linking the How-column aggregation process to the What-column aggregate entity.

payload.statement: The system shall trigger weekly earnings aggregation decision logic upon detection of a weekly period boundary state transition.
payload.rationale: Specifies the logical temporal condition (When-column event) that initiates the aggregation process (How-column), establishing the state-driven execution model without algorithmic detail.

payload.statement: The system shall execute a task availability reset process at each weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical process capability that restores task availability states at the end of each weekly period, ensuring cyclical renewal of task opportunities.

payload.statement: The system shall recognize weekly period boundary occurrences as temporal events.
payload.rationale: Establishes the logical event detection capability that identifies when a weekly period concludes, providing the temporal reference point for cycle-dependent behavior.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a weekly task availability reset cycle.
payload.rationale: Defines the recurring temporal lifecycle structure that governs the periodicity of task availability state management, establishing the logical cycle within which reset events occur.

payload.statement: The system shall transition task instances to reset availability state when a weekly cycle boundary is triggered.
payload.rationale: Specifies the causal relationship between the temporal trigger and the resulting state transition, defining the event-driven logic that initiates availability reset behavior.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association among child entity instances, completed task instances, and earnings data instances within the reporting view model.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship among three logical entities in the reporting view model, enabling cross-entity analytical queries.

payload.statement: The system shall generate a reporting view that correlates child entity instances with their associated completed task instances and corresponding earnings data.
payload.rationale: Specifies the logical view generation behavior that integrates the three entity types into a cohesive reporting perspective.

payload.statement: The logical model shall maintain a temporal task record entity for historical reference of tasks.

payload.fit_criteria: Each task maintains a historical record accessible for reference

payload.statement: The logical model shall maintain a task completion record entity that links child entities to completed tasks.

payload.statement: The logical model shall correlate child entities, completed tasks, and earnings data through a reporting view association.

payload.fit_criteria: Reporting views establish associations between child entities and their assigned task data

payload.statement: The logical model shall provide a function for persisting and retrieving temporal task records upon task completion.

payload.fit_criteria: Temporal task records are persisted and retrievable when children complete tasks

payload.statement: The logical model shall maintain child entities as participants in the reporting view correlating tasks and earnings.

payload.fit_criteria: Parent entities are maintained as data structures linked to tasks and earnings

payload.statement: The logical model shall execute task availability reset process logic at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Refines parent task definition capability to the logical level by introducing the weekly reset lifecycle process that maintains task availability.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability states are reset automatically at each weekly cycle boundary

payload.statement: The logical model shall generate reporting views that correlate child entities with completed task and earnings data.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task completion records in a relational database schema with foreign key relationships linking child identifiers to completed task instance identifiers.
payload.rationale: Realizes the logical task completion record entity as a physical relational database schema with explicit foreign key constraints.

payload.statement: The task completion record table shall include a child_id column as a non-null foreign key referencing the child entity table.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical association between task completion records and child identifiers as a physical database schema column constraint.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain task records in a persistent storage layer that supports temporal data retention beyond the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: Specifies the physical storage layer requirement to support historical data retention without automatic archival or deletion.
payload.fit_criteria: Task records shall remain accessible for retrieval for a minimum of 12 months.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a scheduled batch process component that executes weekly earnings aggregation calculations at the weekly period boundary.
payload.rationale: Realizes the logical aggregation calculation process as a physical scheduled batch component with timing constraints.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings aggregation process shall complete within 5 minutes of the weekly boundary trigger.

payload.statement: The system shall persist weekly earnings calculation results in a relational database table with one record per child per weekly period.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical weekly earnings aggregate entity as a physical database table structure with child and period identifiers.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a scheduled task reset service that executes at the weekly cycle boundary to update all task status values to available.
payload.rationale: Realizes the logical task availability reset process as a physical scheduled service component with database update operations.
payload.fit_criteria: Task reset process shall update all task records within 1 minute of the weekly boundary.

payload.statement: The task reset service shall enforce a weekly recurrence schedule that triggers task state transitions at consistent weekly intervals.
payload.rationale: Establishes the physical scheduling constraint governing the task reset service execution frequency.
payload.fit_criteria: Task reset shall execute every 7 days ± 5 minutes from the configured weekly boundary time.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a weekly cycle scheduler component that triggers both task reset and earnings aggregation processes at the configured weekly boundary time.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the two weekly cycle constraints into a single physical scheduler component that coordinates both processes.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler shall trigger both processes within 1 minute of the weekly boundary time.

payload.fit_criteria: Reporting view queries shall return results within 2 seconds for datasets up to 10,000 records.

payload.statement: The system shall insert a new task completion record into the database when processing a task completion claim event from a child.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical task completion record creation as a physical database insert operation triggered by an application event.
payload.fit_criteria: Task completion record shall be persisted within 500ms of receiving the completion claim.

payload.statement: The system shall implement database indexes on the task record table to support efficient historical retrieval queries.
payload.rationale: Adds physical database optimization structures to support the logical requirement for historical task record retrieval.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical task queries shall return results within 1 second for date ranges spanning up to 12 months.

payload.statement: The earnings aggregation batch process shall execute a SQL aggregate query that sums reward values from task completion records grouped by child identifier and filtered by the weekly period date range.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical earnings calculation as a physical SQL aggregation operation with specific grouping and filtering clauses.

payload.statement: The weekly cycle scheduler component shall detect weekly boundary time occurrences and invoke the earnings aggregation batch process as a scheduled task.
payload.rationale: Realizes the logical trigger mechanism as a physical scheduler component that invokes the aggregation process on a time-based event.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler shall invoke earnings aggregation within 1 minute of the configured weekly boundary time.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a scheduled task reset batch process that executes at each configured weekly cycle boundary time.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical task reset process as a physical scheduled batch component with time-based execution.
payload.fit_criteria: Task reset process shall execute within 1 minute of the weekly boundary time.

payload.statement: The weekly cycle scheduler component shall detect and log weekly period boundary occurrences as discrete temporal events for process triggering.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical recognition of weekly boundaries as a physical event detection and logging capability within the scheduler.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a weekly task availability reset cycle using a scheduler service configured with a 7-day recurrence interval.
payload.rationale: Realizes the logical weekly cycle as a physical scheduler service configuration with a specific recurrence pattern.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler shall maintain a 7-day recurrence interval with ±5 minute accuracy.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a historical task record table in the relational database schema to support temporal queries.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical temporal task record entity as a physical database table with temporal attributes.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a task completion record table in the relational database schema with foreign key columns linking child_id to the child table and task_id to the task table.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a database view or query interface that joins the child, task completion, and earnings tables to expose correlated reporting data.

payload.statement: The system shall implement data access layer methods for inserting task completion records and querying historical task records from the database.
payload.rationale: Realizes the logical persistence and retrieval functions as physical data access layer API methods.

payload.statement: The reporting view database schema shall include the child table as a primary entity with join relationships to task and earnings tables.
payload.rationale: Specifies the physical database schema structure placing child entities as the primary key in reporting view joins.

payload.statement: The database schema shall include a parent table with foreign key relationships to task and earnings data tables.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical parent entity association as physical database table relationships.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a task reset batch service that executes SQL update statements to set task status to available at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Realizes the logical task reset process as a physical batch service executing database update operations on a schedule.

payload.statement: The system shall implement a reporting API or database view that executes join queries across child, task completion, and earnings tables to generate correlated reports.
payload.rationale: Implements the logical reporting view generation as a physical API service or database view executing multi-table queries.
payload.fit_criteria: Reporting queries shall return results within 2 seconds for datasets up to 10,000 records.

payload.statement: The system shall be deployed on the target operating system version node.
payload.rationale: Establishes the physical deployment target environment at the platform level, specifying where the system will be hosted in terms of operating system infrastructure.

payload.statement: The system shall comply with operating system version compatibility constraints.
payload.rationale: Constrains the system to operate within defined operating system version boundaries, ensuring compatibility and supportability across the deployment environment.

payload.statement: The task completion record table shall define a child_id column of type INTEGER with a NOT NULL constraint and a FOREIGN KEY constraint referencing the child table's primary key column.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact column definition, data type, and constraint syntax for the child_id foreign key linking task completion records to child entities.
payload.fit_criteria: Column definition includes INTEGER type, NOT NULL constraint, and FOREIGN KEY reference to child table primary key.

payload.statement: The task completion record table shall define a task_id column of type INTEGER with a NOT NULL constraint and a FOREIGN KEY constraint referencing the task table's primary key column.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact column definition, data type, and constraint syntax for the task_id foreign key linking task completion records to task entities.
payload.fit_criteria: Column definition includes INTEGER type, NOT NULL constraint, and FOREIGN KEY reference to task table primary key.

payload.statement: The task completion record table shall include a completion_timestamp column of type TIMESTAMP with a NOT NULL constraint to record the exact date and time of task completion.
payload.rationale: Defines the precise temporal field required to capture task completion event timing for linking and aggregation operations.
payload.fit_criteria: Column definition includes TIMESTAMP type and NOT NULL constraint.

payload.statement: The child_id column in the task completion record table shall be defined with ON DELETE CASCADE referential action to automatically remove completion records when the referenced child entity is deleted.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact referential integrity action to maintain consistency when parent child entities are removed.
payload.fit_criteria: Foreign key constraint includes ON DELETE CASCADE clause.

payload.statement: The task completion record table shall define an indexed primary key column completion_id of type SERIAL or AUTO_INCREMENT INTEGER to uniquely identify each completion record.
payload.rationale: Provides the exact primary key definition needed to uniquely identify task completion records and support efficient foreign key references.
payload.fit_criteria: Primary key column is auto-incrementing integer type with unique constraint.

payload.statement: The database schema shall configure task completion and earnings tables with a retention policy that prevents automatic deletion of records for a minimum of 365 days from the completion_timestamp value.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact temporal retention constraint value to support historical data retention beyond current weekly periods.
payload.fit_criteria: Retention policy configured for minimum 365 days; no automatic deletion occurs before this threshold.

payload.statement: The task completion record table and earnings table shall be configured with the InnoDB storage engine (MySQL) or equivalent ACID-compliant engine to ensure durable persistence across system restarts.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact storage engine requirement to guarantee persistent retention of temporal task data.
payload.fit_criteria: Tables use InnoDB or equivalent ACID-compliant engine with durable write guarantees.

payload.statement: The weekly earnings aggregation batch process shall be scheduled using a cron expression '0 0 * * 0' or equivalent scheduler configuration to execute at 00:00 hours every Sunday.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact cron schedule expression and execution time defining the weekly period boundary for earnings aggregation.
payload.fit_criteria: Batch process executes at 00:00 Sunday as configured by cron expression '0 0 * * 0'.

payload.statement: The weekly earnings aggregation batch process shall execute a transactional stored procedure or batch script that computes aggregated earnings and commits results atomically to prevent partial updates.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact transactional execution mechanism ensuring atomicity of weekly earnings calculation and persistence.
payload.fit_criteria: Aggregation executes within a database transaction; all inserts commit together or roll back on failure.

payload.statement: The weekly earnings table shall define columns child_id (INTEGER, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY to child table), week_start_date (DATE, NOT NULL), week_end_date (DATE, NOT NULL), and total_earnings (DECIMAL(10,2), NOT NULL, CHECK >= 0) with a composite primary key on (child_id, week_start_date).
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact table schema, column data types, constraints, and primary key definition for weekly earnings records.
payload.fit_criteria: Table schema matches specified columns, types, constraints, and composite primary key definition.

payload.statement: The task reset service shall execute a SQL UPDATE statement 'UPDATE task SET status = 'available' WHERE status IN ('claimed', 'completed')' at the configured weekly cycle boundary time.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact SQL statement and status transition logic executed by the task reset service at weekly boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: UPDATE statement sets all non-available tasks to 'available' status.

payload.statement: The task reset service shall be invoked by a scheduler configured with a recurrence interval of exactly 604800 seconds (7 days) with the first execution aligned to the configured weekly boundary timestamp.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact recurrence interval in seconds and alignment requirement enforcing weekly cycle consistency.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler configuration specifies 604800 second interval; executions occur at consistent weekly timestamps.

payload.statement: The weekly cycle scheduler component shall be implemented as a scheduled task service (e.g., Spring @Scheduled, Quartz scheduler, or OS cron daemon) configured with fixedDelay or cron expression triggering at the weekly boundary time.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact scheduler framework and configuration mechanism used to trigger both task reset and earnings aggregation at weekly boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler uses specified framework with configuration triggering both processes at weekly boundary.

payload.statement: The weekly cycle scheduler component shall invoke the task reset service and earnings aggregation batch process sequentially in a single scheduled execution, with task reset completing before earnings aggregation begins.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact execution sequence and ordering constraint ensuring task reset completes before earnings are calculated.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler invokes task reset; upon completion, invokes earnings aggregation within same scheduled execution.

payload.statement: The reporting database view shall be defined as a SQL VIEW named 'vw_child_task_earnings_report' joining child table (c), task_completion table (tc), and weekly_earnings table (we) on c.child_id = tc.child_id = we.child_id with columns child_id, child_name, task_name, completion_timestamp, reward_value, week_start_date, and total_earnings.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact view name, join conditions, source tables, and output column set for the correlated reporting view.
payload.fit_criteria: View definition includes specified name, three-table join on child_id, and listed columns.

payload.statement: The system shall execute an INSERT statement 'INSERT INTO task_completion (child_id, task_id, completion_timestamp, reward_value) VALUES (?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, ?)' when a task completion claim event is received, using parameterized values from the event payload.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact SQL INSERT statement, parameter binding, and timestamp function used to persist task completion records upon event receipt.
payload.fit_criteria: INSERT statement executes with parameterized child_id, task_id, and reward_value; completion_timestamp set to current time.

payload.statement: The task_completion table shall define a composite non-clustered index on columns (child_id, completion_timestamp) to support efficient retrieval of completion history ordered by time.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact index columns and type to optimize historical query performance for child-scoped temporal retrieval.
payload.fit_criteria: Non-clustered index exists on (child_id, completion_timestamp) columns.

payload.statement: The task_completion table shall define a non-clustered index on the completion_timestamp column to support efficient date range filtering queries.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact single-column index to optimize temporal filtering for weekly period queries.
payload.fit_criteria: Non-clustered index exists on completion_timestamp column.

payload.statement: The earnings aggregation batch process shall execute the SQL statement 'SELECT child_id, SUM(reward_value) AS total_earnings FROM task_completion WHERE completion_timestamp >= ? AND completion_timestamp < ? GROUP BY child_id' with week_start_date and week_end_date parameters defining the weekly period date range.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact SQL aggregate query, grouping clause, date range filter, and parameterization used to compute weekly earnings.
payload.fit_criteria: SQL statement matches specified SELECT with SUM aggregation, date range WHERE clause, and GROUP BY child_id.

payload.statement: The weekly cycle scheduler component shall compute the next weekly boundary time by evaluating the expression 'next_sunday_midnight = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL ((7 - DAYOFWEEK(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)) % 7) DAY' and schedule the earnings aggregation batch process to execute at that timestamp.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact algorithmic calculation for detecting weekly boundary occurrences and scheduling batch execution.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler calculates next Sunday midnight using specified interval expression and schedules batch execution at that time.

payload.statement: The task reset batch process shall be configured as a scheduled job with cron expression '0 0 * * 0' executing at 00:00 hours every Sunday at the weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact cron schedule configuration and execution time for the task reset batch process at weekly boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduled job uses cron expression '0 0 * * 0' executing at Sunday 00:00.

payload.statement: The weekly cycle scheduler component shall write a log entry with severity INFO, message 'Weekly period boundary detected at [timestamp]', and structured fields {event_type: 'weekly_boundary', boundary_timestamp: [ISO8601 timestamp]} whenever a weekly boundary occurrence is detected.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact log message format, severity level, structured field names, and timestamp format for weekly boundary event logging.
payload.fit_criteria: Log entry contains specified message format, INFO severity, and structured fields with ISO8601 timestamp.

payload.statement: The task availability reset scheduler service shall be configured with a fixedRate parameter value of 604800000 milliseconds (7 days) or cron expression '0 0 * * 0' to enforce weekly recurrence.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact scheduler configuration parameter value in milliseconds or cron syntax for 7-day recurrence interval.
payload.fit_criteria: Scheduler configured with fixedRate=604800000 or cron='0 0 * * 0'.

payload.statement: The historical task record table shall define columns task_id (INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY), task_name (VARCHAR(255), NOT NULL), description (TEXT), reward_value (DECIMAL(10,2), NOT NULL), created_date (TIMESTAMP, NOT NULL), and archived_date (TIMESTAMP, NULL) to support temporal queries.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact column schema, data types, and constraints for the historical task record table enabling temporal query support.
payload.fit_criteria: Table schema includes specified columns with listed data types and constraints.

payload.statement: The task_completion table shall be defined with columns completion_id (INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY, AUTO_INCREMENT), child_id (INTEGER, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES child(child_id)), task_id (INTEGER, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES task(task_id)), completion_timestamp (TIMESTAMP, NOT NULL), and reward_value (DECIMAL(10,2), NOT NULL, CHECK >= 0).
payload.rationale: Provides complete detailed schema definition with exact column names, types, constraints, and foreign key references for the task completion record table.
payload.fit_criteria: Table definition matches all specified columns, types, primary key, foreign keys, and check constraint.

payload.statement: The correlated reporting view or query interface shall be implemented as a SQL VIEW or parameterized stored procedure that executes 'SELECT c.child_id, c.child_name, tc.task_id, t.task_name, tc.completion_timestamp, tc.reward_value, we.week_start_date, we.total_earnings FROM child c LEFT JOIN task_completion tc ON c.child_id = tc.child_id LEFT JOIN task t ON tc.task_id = t.task_id LEFT JOIN weekly_earnings we ON c.child_id = we.child_id' with optional WHERE clause filters for date ranges or child identifiers.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact SQL join structure, source tables, join conditions, and output columns for the correlated reporting interface.
payload.fit_criteria: View or procedure executes specified SELECT statement with LEFT JOINs on child, task_completion, task, and weekly_earnings tables.

payload.statement: The data access layer shall implement a method 'insertTaskCompletion(childId: Integer, taskId: Integer, rewardValue: Decimal): CompletionId' that executes a parameterized INSERT statement into the task_completion table and returns the auto-generated completion_id.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact method signature, parameter types, return type, and SQL operation for inserting task completion records.
payload.fit_criteria: Method signature matches specified name, parameters, and return type; executes INSERT statement.

payload.statement: The data access layer shall implement a method 'queryHistoricalTaskRecords(childId: Integer, startDate: Date, endDate: Date): List<TaskCompletionRecord>' that executes a SELECT statement with parameterized date range filtering on the task_completion table.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact method signature, parameter types, return type, and SQL operation for querying historical task records.
payload.fit_criteria: Method signature matches specified name and parameters; executes SELECT with date range WHERE clause.

payload.statement: The reporting view database schema shall define the child table with columns child_id (INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY), child_name (VARCHAR(255), NOT NULL), and parent_id (INTEGER, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES parent(parent_id)) as the primary entity for join relationships.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact schema structure, columns, data types, and primary/foreign key definitions for the child table serving as the primary join entity.
payload.fit_criteria: Child table schema includes specified columns, types, primary key, and foreign key reference to parent table.

payload.statement: The database schema shall define a parent table with columns parent_id (INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY), parent_name (VARCHAR(255), NOT NULL), and email (VARCHAR(255), UNIQUE, NOT NULL) with foreign key relationships defined in child, task, and weekly_earnings tables referencing parent_id.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact parent table schema, column definitions, constraints, and inbound foreign key relationships from related tables.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent table schema matches specified columns and constraints; child, task, and earnings tables include foreign keys to parent_id.

payload.statement: The task reset batch service shall execute the SQL statement 'UPDATE task SET status = 'available', reset_timestamp = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE status IN ('claimed', 'completed')' within a single database transaction at the weekly cycle boundary time.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact SQL UPDATE statement, status transition logic, timestamp update, and transactional execution requirement for task reset.
payload.fit_criteria: Batch service executes specified UPDATE statement within transaction; all eligible tasks transition to 'available' status.

payload.statement: The reporting API shall expose a REST endpoint 'GET /api/reports/child-earnings?childId={id}&startDate={date}&endDate={date}' that executes the correlated join query and returns JSON-formatted results with fields childId, childName, tasks (array), and totalEarnings.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact REST API endpoint path, query parameters, SQL query execution, and JSON response structure for correlated reporting.
payload.fit_criteria: API endpoint matches specified path and parameters; returns JSON with specified field structure.

payload.statement: The system shall be deployed on iOS devices running iOS version 15.0 or higher.

payload.fit_criteria: Deployment target configuration specifies iOS >= 15.0.

payload.rationale: Specifies exact operating system version, kernel version, and runtime version constraints for operational deployment platform node.
payload.fit_criteria: Deployment environment uses Linux kernel >= 5.4 and OpenJDK >= 17.

payload.rationale: Defines the exact SDK version and deployment target configuration value ensuring iOS platform compatibility.
payload.fit_criteria: Xcode project minimum deployment target set to iOS 15.0; SDK version >= 15.0.

payload.statement: The system deployment configuration shall comply with server operating system compatibility by declaring dependencies on glibc version 2.31 or higher and ensuring all compiled binaries are ELF64 format compatible with Linux kernel 5.4 or higher.
payload.rationale: Specifies exact library version dependencies and binary format requirements ensuring server OS compatibility.
payload.fit_criteria: Dependencies declare glibc >= 2.31; binaries are ELF64 format compatible with kernel >= 5.4.

payload.statement: The system shall execute the task completion linking procedure when a task state transition to completed is detected.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed procedural step that must be executed during task completion processing to establish necessary linkages and state propagation.

payload.statement: The system shall trigger the linking action upon occurrence of the task completion event.
payload.rationale: Defines the event-response contract whereby task completion events cause the execution of linking actions that coordinate downstream task dependencies.

payload.statement: The system shall execute on an iOS mobile platform with a minimum version of iOS 14.0 and target version iOS 15.0 or later.
payload.rationale: Specifies the precise iOS platform version constraint defining the minimum supported runtime environment and target deployment version for mobile execution.
payload.fit_criteria: Deployment package successfully installs and launches on iOS 14.0, iOS 15.0, and iOS 16.0 devices without version compatibility errors.

payload.statement: The operational deployment platform shall provide a Linux-based runtime environment with kernel version 5.4 or later, Python 3.9 or later, and a minimum of 2 CPU cores with 4 GB RAM allocated.
payload.rationale: Defines the precise infrastructure and runtime requirements for the operational deployment node, specifying exact platform versions and minimum resource allocations necessary for system execution.
payload.fit_criteria: Deployment environment meets all specified version and resource thresholds: Linux kernel >= 5.4, Python >= 3.9, CPU >= 2 cores, RAM >= 4 GB.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a Child user role that is authorized to perform task claiming operations.
payload.rationale: Defines the Child user role entity with its operational capability boundary for task claiming; consolidates the role definition (CCI-001) and the actor performing the operations (CCI-005) into a single structural obligation.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a Parent user role that is authorized to perform task definition operations and monetary value assignment operations.
payload.rationale: Defines the Parent user role entity with its operational capability boundaries for task definition and value assignment; consolidates the role responsibility (CCI-002) and the actor definition (CCI-006) into a single structural obligation.

payload.statement: The system shall restrict Child user role task status visibility permissions to a weekly time boundary.
payload.rationale: Specifies an authorization constraint governing the temporal scope of visibility permissions for the Child role; the weekly boundary is the implementable access control rule.

payload.statement: The system shall enable the Parent user role to generate earned pocket money reports for children.
payload.rationale: Specifies the reporting capability obligation assigned to the Parent role; derived from the reporting responsibility portion of CCI-004.

payload.statement: The system shall enable the Parent user role to perform disbursement operations to children.
payload.rationale: Specifies the disbursement capability obligation assigned to the Parent role; derived from the disbursement responsibility portion of CCI-004.

payload.statement: The system shall reset task availability at a weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed timing behavior for task availability reset operations at the weekly operational cycle boundary.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability state transitions occur exactly once per seven-day cycle at the designated boundary timestamp.

payload.statement: The system shall update children's earned totals visibility at a weekly reporting cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Defines the precise timing for earned totals visibility refresh aligned to the weekly reporting cycle.
payload.fit_criteria: Earned totals visibility updates execute at the seven-day reporting cycle boundary with no intermediate updates.

payload.statement: The system shall update task status visibility at a weekly time boundary cycle.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed timing behavior for task status visibility window refresh operations.
payload.fit_criteria: Task status visibility state updates occur at the seven-day time boundary cycle with consistent timing relative to the cycle start.

payload.statement: The system shall retain historical task data for a duration specified by the timing requirement.
payload.rationale: Detailed constraint governing the retention period for historical task data persistence.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: task
payload.description: The business shall maintain a Task as a work unit entity with an associated monetary reward value.

payload.name: reward value
payload.description: The business shall maintain a Task as a work unit entity with an associated monetary reward value.

payload.name: completion status
payload.description: The business shall maintain a completion status for each task indicating whether the task is available or completed.

value_set: Every task completion record contains a non-null child entity identifier
Tables use InnoDB or equivalent ACID-compliant engine with durable write guarantees.

payload.name: ownership relationship
payload.description: The business shall maintain an ownership relationship between each parent and their specified tasks.

payload.name: parent
payload.description: The business shall maintain an ownership relationship between each parent and their specified tasks.

payload.name: child
payload.description: The system shall make task completion claimable by children.

value_set: Foreign key constraint includes ON DELETE CASCADE clause.

payload.name: weekly earnings aggregate
payload.description: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.

payload.surface_term: The system shall associate each task completion record with

payload.name: weekly period boundary
payload.description: The system shall execute a weekly earnings aggregation calculation process at each weekly period boundary.

payload.name: task availability reset
payload.description: The system shall execute a task availability reset process at the weekly cycle boundary to transition all tasks to available state.

value_set: Task state transitions occur only at weekly cycle boundaries
Task reset and earnings calculation both execute at the same weekly period boundary
Task reset shall execute every 7 days ± 5 minutes from the configured weekly boundary time.
Scheduler configuration specifies 604800 second interval; executions occur at consistent weekly timestamps.

payload.surface_term: The system shall enforce a weekly task availability reset cy

payload.name: reporting view
payload.description: The system shall generate a reporting view correlating child entities, completed task records, and earnings data.

payload.name: period identifier
payload.description: The system shall maintain a logical aggregate entity for weekly earnings comprising period identifier, aggregate values, and calculation state.

payload.name: calculation state
payload.description: The system shall maintain a logical aggregate entity for weekly earnings comprising period identifier, aggregate values, and calculation state.

payload.name: batch process
payload.description: The system shall implement a scheduled batch process component that executes weekly earnings aggregation calculations at the weekly period boundary.

payload.name: database index
payload.description: The system shall implement database indexes on the task record table to support efficient historical retrieval queries.

payload.name: aggregate query
payload.description: The earnings aggregation batch process shall execute a SQL aggregate query that sums reward values from task completion records grouped by child identifier and filtered by the weekly period date range.

payload.name: data access layer
payload.description: The system shall implement data access layer methods for inserting task completion records and querying historical task records from the database.

payload.name: operating system
payload.description: The system shall be deployed on the target operating system version node.

value_set: Deployment environment meets all specified version and resource thresholds: Linux kernel >= 5.4, Python >= 3.9, CPU >= 2 cores, RAM >= 4 GB.

payload.name: completion timestamp
payload.description: The task completion record table shall include a completion_timestamp column of type TIMESTAMP with a NOT NULL constraint to record the exact date and time of task completion.

payload.name: database schema
payload.description: The database schema shall configure task completion and earnings tables with a retention policy that prevents automatic deletion of records for a minimum of 365 days from the completion_timestamp value.

value_set: Retention policy configured for minimum 365 days; no automatic deletion occurs before this threshold.

payload.name: log entry
payload.description: The weekly cycle scheduler component shall write a log entry with severity INFO, message 'Weekly period boundary detected at [timestamp]', and structured fields {event_type: 'weekly_boundary', boundary_timestamp: [ISO8601 timestamp]} whenever a weekly boundary occurrence is detected.

payload.name: system
payload.description: The system shall be deployed on iOS devices running iOS version 15.0 or higher.

value_set: Deployment environment uses Linux kernel >= 5.4 and OpenJDK >= 17.
Deployment package successfully installs and launches on iOS 14.0, iOS 15.0, and iOS 16.0 devices without version compatibility errors.

payload.name: system deployment configuration
payload.description: The system deployment configuration shall specify compatibility with iOS SDK version 15.0 or higher in the Xcode project settings with a minimum deployment target of iOS 15.0.

value_set: Xcode project minimum deployment target set to iOS 15.0; SDK version >= 15.0.
Dependencies declare glibc >= 2.31; binaries are ELF64 format compatible with kernel >= 5.4.

payload.name: disbursement
payload.description: The system shall enable the Parent user role to perform disbursement operations to children.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL DataDictionaryEntry elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Section header indicating the conceptual overview of the system follows.
Core business concept establishing the system enables children to claim completion of pocket money earning tasks during a weekly cycle.
Business capability defining that parents specify tasks with associated monetary reward values.
Conceptual relationship rule requiring task completion events to be associated with the completing child entity.
Business capability requiring children view both available and their own completed tasks within the current week.
Business capability defining weekly aggregation reporting of earnings for each child at period end.
Business capability enabling parents to view weekly earnings and completion reports per child for payment reconciliation.
Business rule defining weekly reset cycle where all tasks return to available state.
Business requirement mandating retention and accessibility of task completion history beyond current period.
Describes logical association mechanism linking completed tasks to child entities.
Specifies logical aggregation process calculating weekly earnings at period boundary.
Defines logical reporting view correlating child entities with task completion and earnings data.
Describes logical state transition resetting task availability at weekly cycle boundaries.
Requires logical persistence and retrieval mechanism for temporal task records.
